title: Bridge Essay: Sequence termination cues drive habits via dopamine-mediated credit assignment.
concept: The mechanical mind — neuroscience of automaticity revealing how we become trapped in behavioral loops.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Mechanicality** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** The mechanical mind — neuroscience of automaticity revealing how we become trapped in behavioral loops.

**Sequence termination cues drive habits via dopamine-mediated credit assignment.** - bioRxiv : the preprint server for biology (2025). DOI: 10.1101/2024.10.16.618735. PMID: 39463939. BACKGROUND: Mesolimbic dopamine (DA) neurons are central to cue guided reward seeking and action sequence learning. Yet, the mechanisms by which cue-induced DA neural activity drives goal-directed or habitual sequence execution remain unknown. Mesolimbic dopamine (DA) neurons are central to cue guided reward seeking and action sequence learning.
